workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just came out of consumption trend with Treyarch Global. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-01-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is consumption trend.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:12:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:21:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:26:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-M trap (Rigid coaching frame when direct feedback was the right move); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-M failure mode (Rigid coaching frame when direct feedback was the right move)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-M -- Rigid coaching frame when direct feedback was the right move
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
